IDF Analyzer

Tool Guide

Back to App
Open Input Views Analysis Edit Version Developer Build Update Log

Open and Analyze

Open supports `.idf`, `.imf`, `.epjson`, `.json`, and `.txt` files. Analyze detects IDF or epJSON from the file extension and content.

Input Views

Text

The editable source view for IDF or epJSON text.

JSON

A grouped, object-oriented view of the cached structured EnergyPlus input model.

Table

A spreadsheet-style field table. Value cells can be edited and committed with Enter or blur.

Cached Conversion

Analyze keeps the parsed model and epJSON conversion in memory for fast switching.

Analysis Views

Summary

Object counts, type counts, schedules, unused candidates, and zone cards.

Objects

Searchable object list with index, type, name, and field count.

Schedules

Schedule objects and conservative unused named-object candidates.

Systems

Simple node-to-node HVAC connection diagrams from inlet and outlet node fields.

Edit, Convert, and Export

Remove unused currently targets schedule, material, construction, curve, and table objects that are not referenced by other fields. To IDF and To epJSON convert the current text. Download exports the current editor text.

Version and IDD References

When a Version object is present, EnergyPlus version 22 or newer is accepted. Future version-specific IDD and epJSON schema validation can be added under `resources/energyplus/<major>.<minor>/`.

Developer Build

Use `dev.bat` from the repository root to run setup, tests, builds, and verification through PowerShell with the required execution-policy bypass flags. 

.\dev.bat setup
.\dev.bat test
.\dev.bat build
.\dev.bat verify

Update Log

  • 2026-05-29 Added Text, JSON, and Table input views with cached structured model rendering.
  • 2026-05-29 Guide navigation now stays inside the Wails WebView to avoid external `wails.localhost` connection failures.
  • 2026-05-29 Added IDF/epJSON auto-detection, EnergyPlus 22+ version detection, and conversion workflow.
  • 2026-05-29 Initial guide for open, analysis, edit, and developer build workflow.
